title: Auto-promote first registered user to admin

requirements.text: In the backend registerOrLogin() function, check whether any users exist in the system before creating a new user record. If no users exist (i.e., the caller is the very first user to register), automatically assign them the #admin role and #approved status instead of the default #pending status and #guest role. This bypasses the approval workflow exclusively for the bootstrap case.

quotes: i need to make sure that the very first user who logs into this platform once live, will be set as the main admin
the first user requires approval but there is no way to approve it since there are no active users

acceptanceCriteria: When registerOrLogin() is called and the user store is empty (zero existing users), the new user is created with role = #admin and status = #approved.
When registerOrLogin() is called and at least one user already exists, the new user is created with role = #guest and status = #pending as before.
The first registered user can immediately access admin-only routes and functions (e.g., getAllUsers, updateUserRole, approveUser) without any separate approval step.
Subsequent users still go through the normal pending approval workflow and cannot access admin features until approved by an admin.
No existing approved user's role or status is altered by this change.

constraints: Do not alter the approval workflow for any user registration that occurs when at least one user already exists.
The bootstrap promotion logic must live entirely in the backend registerOrLogin() function — no frontend workarounds.
nonGoals: Adding a separate admin-seeding CLI or script.
Changing the role or status of any already-registered user.
Modifying the frontend authentication or routing logic.
